message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed lever for improving a RACE score. Default to refuted=true when uncertain.

LEVER: Ship-blocker scrub: delete pipeline artifacts that self-incriminate
TARGETS: readability (0.14) / S1 Overall Structure w=0.20; S2 Cohesion w=0.15; L1 Language Clarity w=0.20; F1 Formatting w=0.10 — plus spillover to insight w=0.25 Critical Synthesis (the 'No contradictions were detected by the pipeline' confession) (weight 0.14)
CHANGE: Post-compose sanitizer in the flywheel compose stage: (1) strip all 25 '(also mirrored)' markers from the body; (2) rewrite the 5 dangling section openers (Intro 'Within this framework...', Theory 'This framework...', GenAI 'The study also documents...', the verbless fragment opening Cross-Study Synthesis, 'By contrast' opening Additional Corroborated Findings) so each section opens with a topic sentence; (3) delete the meta boilerplate opener ('Every quantitative claim is span-grounded...') and rewrite Limitations to drop pipeline telemetry language ('No contradictions were detected by the pipeline', 'The telemetry block does not surface...'); (4) merge 'Additional Corroborated Findings' into thematic sections so nothing follows the Conclusion; (5) break each single-paragraph section into 3-5 paragraphs; (6) add the missing final period. NOTE: the brief's 'ings in the last two years' mid-word bug and fact_dedup cross-references do NOT exist in Rank10 — do not chase them.
CLAIMED GAIN: +0.010 to +0.015 (readability 0.377 → ~0.46; ceiling limited by 0.14 weight but this is our worst dim and the fixes are deterministic)
CLAIMED FAILURE MODE: Barely can. Worst case the paragraph-splitting is arbitrary and S2 doesn't move; the marker/fragment deletions are pure upside. Risk of over-scrubbing the Limitations section into vacuity — keep a 2-sentence honest scope note.

ATTACK IT THROUGH THIS LENS:
NOISE: is the claimed gain distinguishable from the measured +/-0.016 noise floor on a single task? If not, it is unfalsifiable as specified.

Context: our report /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md scored 0.4313. Reference /home/polaris/polaris_project/RACE_REFERENCE_task72.md = 0.5000 by definition. SOTA = 0.5265.
Read the files if you need to check a claim. Be adversarial, concrete, and brief.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
9194 report.md
3
3:This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence on the question above. It is organized as a coherent review: an introduction that frames the scope, thematic sections that group the evidence by sub-topic, a cross-study synthesis that surfaces where the findings agree and conflict, and a closing discussion of conclusions and open research gaps. Every quantitative claim is span-grounded to a cited source; claims that could not be verified against the underlying evidence were removed rather than paraphrased.
47:Limitations: The corpus skews heavily toward secondary and synthesized sources, with only 6% of materials classified as T1 primary studies, while T4 and T6 tiers each account for 21% and a further 15% of sources remain of UNKNOWN tier, indicating substantial gaps in high-certainty empirical coverage. No contradictions were detected by the pipeline, so no cross-source disagreement on magnitude, direction, or endpoint is asserted. The telemetry block does not surface an explicit date range for the corpus, which limits the ability to characterize the evidence horizon or identify temporal gaps in source coverage. These distributional and provenance constraints mean the pipeline's synthesis capacity is weighted toward interpretation-layer material rather than original experimental or observational data.

content.text:
I checked the report and the recorded noise measurement.

**The diagnosis is real; the lever is not measurable.**

Facts confirmed in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md`:
- Exactly 25 `(also mirrored)` markers (grep-confirmed).
- Every one of the 11 prose sections is a **single blank-line-free block**: Intro 802w, Theory 819w, Exposure 911w, Skills 984w, etc.
- Line 3 contains `Every quantitative claim is span-grounded...`; line 47 contains `No contradictions were detected by the pipeline` and `The telemetry block does not surface...`.
- `## Additional Corroborated Findings` (669w) does sit after `## Conclusions`.

So the pathologies exist. That is not the question.

**Noise attack (fatal as specified):**

`OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md:305-307` records the only variance measurement this project has: the champion artifact was re-scored *unchanged* and went **0.4447 → 0.4291**, a paired delta of **0.0156**. That is a single paired observation, so σ_diff ≈ 0.016 and single-score σ ≈ 0.011.

The claimed gain is **+0.010 to +0.015** — i.e. the entire claimed effect is **at or below one standard deviation of the re-score noise on the same file**. To detect δ=0.012 against σ_diff=0.016 at 80% power you need ≈ (2.8·0.016/0.012)² ≈ **14 paired scoring runs**. The pipeline scores each report once. Any post-scrub score in roughly [0.415, 0.447] is equally consistent with "the scrub worked" and "the scrub did literally nothing" — the experiment cannot come back negative and cannot come back positive. It is unfalsifiable at the granularity this project measures.

**Second layer of the same problem:** the +0.012 requires readability to move 0.377 → ~0.46, a **+0.083 / +22% relative** jump on one 0.14-weight dim. Sub-dimension scores are *noisier* than the aggregate (the aggregate averages four dims), and the aggregate already moved 0.0156 on a pure re-score. A single dim swinging 0.05-0.08 on re-score alone is entirely expected. The claimed readability delta is inside the per-dim rescore band too.

**"The fixes are deterministic" is false for the parts that would carry the gain.** Only (1) marker strip, (3-partial) sentence deletes, and (6) terminal period are regex-deterministic — and together they change ~40 tokens out of 9,194 words (**0.4% of the text**). The items that could plausibly move S1/S2/L1 — rewriting 5 section openers, splitting 800-word blocks into 3-5 coherent paragraphs, semantically re-homing 669 words of orphan findings into thematic sections — are **LLM rewrite passes**, which add their own run-to-run variance *on top of* judge variance. A single A/B on this bundle is doubly confounded.

**Over-scrub risk is worse than stated.** The "meta boilerplate opener" (line 3) is also the only **roadmap paragraph** in the document ("an introduction that frames the scope, thematic sections..., a cross-study synthesis..., a closing discussion"). Deleting it plausibly *costs* S1 Overall Structure, the largest sub-weight (0.20) the lever is targeting. And item (4) re-homing orphan findings is exactly the routing step that failed and produced "Additional Corroborated Findings" in the first place; forcing it risks topical incoherence in the receiving sections (S2 Cohesion, the same dim being targeted).

**Salvage:** ship the deterministic subset as unconditional pipeline hygiene, never as a measured lever. Strip the 25 markers, cut the two telemetry-confession sentences (keeping the tier/coverage scope note), keep the roadmap sentence, add terminal punctuation, and split paragraphs by a fixed rule (break at every 4th sentence) inside the compose template rather than in a post-hoc sanitizer. Cost is near zero and it removes self-incriminating artifacts that would embarrass any human reader. But do **not** spend a scoring run A/B-ing it, do **not** book +0.010-0.015 against it, and do **not** let a post-scrub score of 0.44 be read as evidence it worked. If it must be tested, fold it into a high-effect lever and measure the bundle.

input.lever: Ship-blocker scrub: delete pipeline artifacts that self-incriminate
input.reasoning:
DIAGNOSIS IS REAL, LEVER IS UNMEASURABLE. Confirmed in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md: exactly 25 '(also mirrored)' markers; all 11 prose sections are single blank-line-free blocks (Intro 802w, Theory 819w, Exposure 911w, Skills 984w); line 3 has the 'Every quantitative claim is span-grounded' boilerplate; line 47 has 'No contradictions were detected by the pipeline' and 'The telemetry block does not surface...'; 'Additional Corroborated Findings' (669w) does follow Conclusions. So the pathologies exist.

NOISE KILL: OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md:305-307 records the project's only variance measurement — the champion artifact re-scored UNCHANGED went 0.4447 -> 0.4291, paired delta 0.0156 (sigma_diff ~0.016, single-score sigma ~0.011). The claimed gain (+0.010 to +0.015) is entirely AT OR BELOW one SD of same-file re-score noise. Detecting delta=0.012 against sigma_diff=0.016 at 80% power needs ~14 paired scoring runs; the pipeline scores each report once. Any post-scrub score in ~[0.415, 0.447] is equally consistent with 'worked' and 'did nothing.' The experiment cannot return a negative or a positive. Unfalsifiable as specified.

SECOND LAYER: +0.012 overall requires readability 0.377 -> ~0.46, a +0.083 / +22% relative jump on one 0.14-weight dim. Per-dimension scores are noisier than the aggregate (aggregate averages 4 dims), and the aggregate already moved 0.0156 on a pure re-score. A single dim swinging 0.05-0.08 on re-score alone is expected. The claimed readability delta sits inside the per-dim rescore band too.

'DETERMINISTIC' IS FALSE FOR THE LOAD-BEARING PARTS: only the marker strip, the two sentence deletes, and the terminal period are regex-deterministic — together ~40 tokens out of 9,194 words (0.4% of the text). Expecting a 22% relative readability lift from deleting 25 parentheticals is not credible. The items that could actually move S1/S2/L1 (rewriting 5 openers, splitting 800-word blocks into 3-5 coherent paragraphs, semantically re-homing 669 orphan words) are LLM rewrite passes that add their OWN run-to-run variance on top of judge variance — a single A/B is doubly confounded.

OVER-SCRUB RISK IS WORSE THAN THE LEVER ADMITS: the 'meta boilerplate opener' on line 3 is also the document's only ROADMAP paragraph ('an introduction that frames the scope, thematic sections..., a cross-study synthesis..., a closing discussion'). Deleting it plausibly COSTS S1 Overall Structure — the largest sub-weight (0.20) the lever targets. And step (4), re-homing orphan findings into thematic sections, is exactly the routing step whose failure produced 'Additional Corroborated Findings'; forcing it risks degrading S2 Cohesion, another targeted dim.
input.salvage: Ship the deterministic subset as unconditional pipeline hygiene, never as a measured lever: regex-strip the 25 '(also mirrored)' markers, cut the two telemetry-confession sentences (keep a 2-sentence honest tier/coverage scope note), KEEP the roadmap sentence (drop only the 'span-grounded' clause), add terminal punctuation, and split paragraphs by a fixed rule (break every ~4 sentences) inside the compose template rather than a post-hoc sanitizer. Cost is near zero and it removes artifacts that would embarrass any human reader. But do NOT spend a scoring run A/B-ing it, do NOT book +0.010-0.015 against it, and do NOT read a post-scrub 0.44 as evidence it worked. If it must be tested, fold it into a high-effect lever and measure the bundle, or run n>=14 paired scores.
